alternative_approaches: Changing `validate_type_code` itself to accept all GTS domain prefixes would be broader and risky because group/type code and parent validation currently rely on its RG-prefix guarantee.
Skipping validation for `allowed_membership_types` entirely would fix external prefixes but would regress the explicit empty and length checks requested for membership codes.
json.fix_description: Add a separate validation helper for allowed membership type codes, for example `validation::validate_membership_type_code`, that trims/lowercases consistently and rejects only empty codes and codes longer than 1024 characters. Do not require `RG_TYPE_PREFIX` in that helper. Replace only the two `allowed_membership_types` loops in `TypeService::create_type` and `TypeService::update_type` to call the new membership helper. Leave `validate_type_code` unchanged for resource-group type codes and `allowed_parent_types`, so RG type creation, parent validation, and group service validation keep their current RG-prefix contract. Keep the existing repository `resolve_ids` checks unchanged so membership entries still have to refer to registered type rows. Cover the behavior with the focused create/update tests in `type_service_test.rs` that seed a registered external code and assert it round-trips through `allowed_membership_types`; also add or keep checks that empty and >1024 membership codes still fail.
json.reasoning: The reproduction shows both public service paths fail before persistence with the RG-prefix validation error even though the external membership code was registered in `gts_type`. In the code, parent and membership validation share the same `validate_type_code` helper, but only parent/resource-group type codes should be constrained to `gts.cf.core.rg.type.v1~`. Membership codes are later resolved by `type_repo.resolve_ids`, which is the correct cross-domain registration check and does not need the RG namespace prefix. Splitting the cheap format validation for membership entries from the RG-specific validator removes the bad prefix requirement while preserving empty/length rejection and all existing existence checks.
json.root_cause: `allowed_membership_types` are membership resource type codes, but `TypeService::create_type` and `TypeService::update_type` validate them with `validation::validate_type_code`. That helper is intentionally scoped to resource-group type registry codes and enforces `RG_TYPE_PREFIX` in `modules/system/resource-group/resource-group/src/domain/validation.rs:20-44`. The offending call sites are the membership loops in `modules/system/resource-group/resource-group/src/domain/type_service.rs:75-82` and `modules/system/resource-group/resource-group/src/domain/type_service.rs:228-230`, so external registered domain types fail before the repository existence check can accept them.
json.summary_markdown:
## Bug
`TypeService::create_type` and `TypeService::update_type` reject registered external domain type codes in `allowed_membership_types`, such as `gts.cf.core.idp.user.v1~...`, because they apply the resource-group type registry prefix validator.

## Cause
`validation::validate_type_code` enforces `gts.cf.core.rg.type.v1~` and is correctly used for RG type codes and parent types. The membership loops in `type_service.rs` reuse that RG-specific helper, so external membership types are rejected before `type_repo.resolve_ids` can verify that the type row exists.

## Fix
Add a membership-specific validation helper in `validation.rs` that preserves normalization plus empty and 1024-character length rejection but does not require the RG prefix. Use it only for `allowed_membership_types` in `create_type` and `update_type`. Keep parent/type validation and `add_membership`/`remove_membership` unchanged.

## Files
- `modules/system/resource-group/resource-group/src/domain/validation.rs`
- `modules/system/resource-group/resource-group/src/domain/type_service.rs`
- `modules/system/resource-group/resource-group/tests/type_service_test.rs`

## Confidence
0.91
json.summary_title: Allow external allowed-membership type codes in resource-group type management
